title: Xenial Earthy Irish Callaloo Stew
description: A hearty, earthy Irish stew featuring callaloo, perfect for a lazy Sunday, made with katsuramuki-cut vegetables and served in an agave leaf vessel, this dish is FODMAP friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shoulder with garlic free seasoning, sea salt, and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the katsuramuki cut carrots and potatoes, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the chopped callaloo and stir to combine.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Serve the stew in agave leaves, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
